soc_ocv.label: SOC-OCV / slow curve
soc_ocv.intent: Derive low-rate charge and discharge voltage curves for OCV and EMF-oriented interpretation and modelling support.

steps.name: Standard-cycle precharge
steps.details: Charge the cell as described in the standard cycle within 48 h before the slow curve.

steps.name: Thermal acclimation
steps.details: Acclimate the cell at {target_temperature_c} C and rest for {rest_minutes} minutes before the slow curve.

steps.name: Slow discharge
steps.details: Discharge at {discharge_c_rate}C to {discharge_cutoff_v} V while preserving the fine logging cadence.

steps.name: Slow recharge
steps.details: Recharge at {charge_c_rate}C to {charge_voltage_v} V and complete the CV taper to C/25.
steps.deviation_note: The extracted handbook text is truncated, so the symmetric recharge completion remains a planner-added completion that must be reviewed.

steps.name: OCV and EMF reconstruction
steps.details: Average the slow charge and discharge curves to derive the working OCV or EMF estimate and ICA or DVA-ready exports.

data_acquisition: Measure every minute, every 1 mV change, and every 0.2 K change.
Run at 25 +/- 2 C after sufficient thermal acclimation.

graphs: Slow charge and discharge curves vs capacity
tables: Cutoff voltages, rest times, and logging settings

planner_notes: The PDF extraction truncates the white paper text after the slow-discharge bullet. The symmetric recharge step is added here as a planner completion and must be reviewed before release.

strict_reference_policy.summary: Use the handbook chapter as the primary executable reference. Literature may complement theory or interpretation but should not silently override the handbook sequence.
tailorable_fields: clarified recharge completion after explicit review

capacity_test.label: Capacity test
capacity_test.intent: Measure capacity and energy content versus C-rate and temperature while collecting voltage and thermal behaviour useful for direct evaluation and model checking.
required_equipment: Battery cell tester with sufficient current headroom
Optional forced air flow and heat camera

steps.name: Initial state preparation
steps.details: If the standard cycle was not completed within the previous 48 h, perform a standard-cycle discharge and recharge first.

steps.name: Pre-discharge rest
steps.details: Rest for {rest_minutes} minutes before the main discharge.

steps.name: Constant-current discharge
steps.details: Discharge at {discharge_c_rate}C to {discharge_cutoff_v} V at the selected chamber temperature.

steps.name: Optional CV discharge
steps.details: Optionally continue at the lower-voltage hold until C/25 to recover the C/25 capacity component.

steps.name: Pre-charge rest
steps.details: Rest for {rest_minutes} minutes before recharge.

steps.name: CC-CV recharge
steps.details: Charge at {charge_c_rate}C to {charge_voltage_v} V and continue CV until C/25.

steps.name: Repeat across matrix
steps.details: Repeat the discharge and charge block for each selected C-rate and temperature.

data_acquisition: Measure every minute, every 10 mV change, every 0.05 C change, and every 0.2 K change.
Wait at least two hours after changing chamber temperature before starting a discharge.

tables: Test matrix of rates and temperatures

planner_notes: The white paper defines an extended test matrix. The planner may narrow that matrix to the requested chemistry, equipment, and project need.

strict_reference_policy.summary: Use the handbook chapter as the primary executable reference. Matrix narrowing is allowed only after explicit review and should preserve the core block structure.

pulse_hppc.label: Pulse test / HPPC
pulse_hppc.intent: Characterize charge and discharge pulse behaviour versus SOC and temperature for power capability analysis and equivalent-circuit parametrization.

steps.name: Climate-chamber acclimation
steps.details: Set the chamber to 23 C and rest for 3 h before the initial full charge.

steps.name: Reference full charge
steps.details: Charge per datasheet to cut-off voltage and continue CV until the current falls below C/25 or C/50 or until 2 h.

steps.name: Reference capacity discharge
steps.details: Rest 30 min, then discharge at 0.5C to the lower cut-off to define actual usable capacity.

steps.name: Return to full SOC
steps.details: Recharge with the same CC-CV method before each temperature sweep.

steps.name: SOC staircase
steps.details: Set chamber to {target_temperature_c} C, rest 6 h, then step the cell by 5% DoD (10% SOC) increments with 1 h rests.

steps.name: Discharge pulse
steps.details: Apply a {discharge_c_rate}C discharge pulse for 18 s or until the lower voltage limit is reached, followed by a 60 s rest.

steps.name: Charge pulse
steps.details: Apply a {charge_c_rate}C charge pulse for 18 s or until the upper voltage limit is reached, followed by a 60 s rest.

steps.name: Repeat across SOC, rate, and temperature
steps.details: Repeat the pulse pair at the selected current rates until 10% SOC, then repeat for each selected temperature.

data_acquisition: Rest segments: measure every minute or every 20 mV change or every 0.5 C change.
Charge and discharge blocks: measure every 5 s or every 20 mV change or every 0.5 C change.
Pulse segments: measure every 10 to 20 ms if possible, or at the fastest tester-supported sampling rate.

graphs: Current and voltage trace for each pulse
R_2s, R_10s, and R_18s vs SOC and temperature
tables: Pulse matrix by temperature, SOC, and current rate

planner_notes: The white paper allows a shortened variant when time is limited. The planner can reduce rate count or widen the SOC step after review.

strict_reference_policy.summary: Use the handbook chapter as the primary executable reference. Reduced rate count or widened SOC spacing must be treated as explicit reviewed deviations.

cycle_life.label: Cycle life test
cycle_life.intent: Assess lifetime under selected use conditions by varying temperature, current or power profile, and voltage window while periodically checking reference performance.
required_equipment: Battery cell tester with current headroom for periodic reference tests

steps.name: Design the ageing matrix
steps.details: Select the temperature, current or power profile, and voltage window combinations relevant to the project objective.

steps.name: Preconditioning and baseline
steps.details: Run the preconditioning test, then execute the initial reference performance test set at room temperature.

steps.name: Thermal stabilization
steps.details: Stabilize the cell at {target_temperature_c} C for 8 h before the ageing profile starts.

steps.name: Ageing block
steps.details: Run the selected ageing profile for {cycle_count} cycles or the agreed throughput block, within the approved voltage window.

steps.name: Periodic check-up
steps.details: Interrupt the ageing profile at the agreed cadence for standard cycle, capacity, and pulse reference tests.

steps.name: End-of-life decision
steps.details: Stop when the project end-of-life criterion is reached, often 80% SOH unless the project defines otherwise.

data_acquisition: Use a logging cadence that preserves the ageing profile, reference checks, and temperature excursions.
Store reference performance test outputs against equivalent cycle count or Ah throughput.

graphs: Capacity or SOH vs equivalent cycles / Ah throughput
Resistance or DCIR vs life fraction
Temperature trend during the ageing block

planner_notes: The white paper defines a DOE-style framework rather than one single executable protocol. The plan must be finalized per project.

strict_reference_policy.summary: Use the handbook chapter as the primary planning frame. Project-specific matrix design is expected, but baseline, periodic-check, and end-of-life checkpoints remain mandatory review anchors.
